label: correctness_bug_off_by_one
summary: The overall structure is correct — validation, response shape, and defaults all satisfy their respective criteria — but the skip formula on line 19 (`skip: page * pageSize`) is definitively wrong for 1-indexed pages. With page=1, skip evaluates to 20, silently discarding the first page and shifting every subsequent page one full page forward. This directly violates AC#3 and means no caller receives the data they requested. The fix is a single character change: `(page - 1) * pageSize`. Additionally, the test file listed in filesChanged was not included in the diff, so test correctness cannot be verified; given the bug, any test asserting page=1 behavior would either catch it (and be failing) or encode the wrong offset (and be wrong). The diff must be revised before merge.

findings.description: skip: page * pageSize is wrong for 1-indexed pagination — with page=1 it skips 20 rows and returns the second page's data instead of the first.
findings.suggestion: Change to skip: (page - 1) * pageSize so that page=1 yields skip=0.

findings.description: Completion signal lists tests/routes/items.test.ts as changed but no test diff was provided; cannot verify that tests cover the page=1 boundary or that they aren't asserting the buggy skip offset.
findings.suggestion: Include the test diff in the next submission; add at minimum a test asserting page=1 returns the first N items (offset 0) and page=2 returns the next N.

fullReview.summary: The overall structure is correct — validation, response shape, and defaults all satisfy their respective criteria — but the skip formula on line 19 (`skip: page * pageSize`) is definitively wrong for 1-indexed pages. With page=1, skip evaluates to 20, silently discarding the first page and shifting every subsequent page one full page forward. This directly violates AC#3 and means no caller receives the data they requested. The fix is a single character change: `(page - 1) * pageSize`. Additionally, the test file listed in filesChanged was not included in the diff, so test correctness cannot be verified; given the bug, any test asserting page=1 behavior would either catch it (and be failing) or encode the wrong offset (and be wrong). The diff must be revised before merge.

criteriaAssessment.evidence: req.query.page and req.query.pageSize are parsed via parseInt on lines 9–10.
req.query.pageSize ?? '20' supplies the default before parseInt.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Page numbers are 1-indexed (page=1 returns first page)
criteriaAssessment.evidence: skip: page * pageSize evaluates to 20 when page=1, skipping the first page entirely. The correct expression is (page - 1) * pageSize.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Response includes { items, totalCount, page, pageSize, totalPages }
criteriaAssessment.evidence: res.json wraps all five fields; totalPages is computed as Math.ceil(totalCount / pageSize).
isNaN and boundary checks (page < 1, pageSize < 1, pageSize > 100) are present and return res.status(400).
